### Format Sandbox Timeout Error (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.3/errors/page.mdx

A function to format sandbox timeout errors. It takes a message string and returns a TimeoutError object. This is useful for standardizing how timeout errors are represented.

```typescript
function formatSandboxTimeoutError(message: string): TimeoutError {
  return new TimeoutError(message);
}
// Defined in errors.ts:2
```

--------------------------------

### Python Filesystem: Write multiple files

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.3.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Writes a list of file entries to the filesystem. Creates files if they don't exist and overwrites existing ones. Errors if directories are missing.

```python
from typing import List, Optional
from e2b.api.users import Username
from e2b.api.filesystem import EntryInfo, WriteEntry

class Filesystem:
    @overload
    def write(self, files: List[WriteEntry],
              user: Optional[Username] = "user",
              request_timeout: Optional[float] = None) -> List[EntryInfo]:
        # Writes a list of files to the filesystem.
        # When writing to a file that doesn't exist, the file will get created.
        # When writing to a file that already exists, the file will get overwritten.
        # When writing to a file that's in a directory that doesn't exist, you'll get an error.
        # Arguments:
        # - `files`: list of files to write
        # - `user`: Run the operation as this user
        # - `request_timeout`: Timeout for the request
        # Returns:
        # Information about the written files
        pass
```

--------------------------------

### Define SandboxError Class (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.2/errors/page.mdx

Defines the base SandboxError class, intended for general sandbox-related errors. It serves as a parent class for more specific error types. Errors are instantiated with a message string.

```typescript
class SandboxError extends Error {
  constructor(message: any) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "SandboxError";
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### Check Existence API

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.1.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Verifies if a file or directory exists at a given path.

```APIDOC
## GET /fs/exists

### Description
Checks whether a file or directory exists at the specified path.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
`/fs/exists`

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **path** (str) - Required - The path to the file or directory to check.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - Run the operation as this user. Defaults to "user".
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **exists** (bool) - `True` if the file or directory exists, `False` otherwise.

#### Response Example
{
  "exists": true
}
```

--------------------------------

### Write Multiple Files

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.3.3/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Writes a list of files to the filesystem. Creates files if they don't exist or overwrites existing ones. Will error if parent directories do not exist.

```APIDOC
## POST /filesystem/write-many

### Description
Writes a list of files to the filesystem. Creates files if they do not exist, overwrites them if they do. Will error if parent directories do not exist.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/filesystem/write-many

### Parameters
#### Request Body
- **files** (List[WriteEntry]) - Required - A list of file entries to write.
- **user** (Username) - Optional - The user to perform the operation as. Defaults to "user".
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "files": [
    {
      "path": "/path/to/file1.txt",
      "data": "Content for file 1"
    },
    {
      "path": "/path/to/file2.txt",
      "data": "Content for file 2"
    }
  ]
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **entryInfos** (List[EntryInfo]) - A list of information about the written files.

#### Response Example
```json
[
  {
    "name": "file1.txt",
    "path": "/path/to/file1.txt",
    "type": "FILE",
    "size": 17,
    "modifiedTime": 1678886400000,
    "accessedTime": 1678886400000
  },
  {
    "name": "file2.txt",
    "path": "/path/to/file2.txt",
    "type": "FILE",
    "size": 17,
    "modifiedTime": 1678886400000,
    "accessedTime": 1678886400000
  }
]
```
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Extract Sandbox Error from Response

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.5.0/messaging/page.mdx

The `extractError()` function takes a `Response` object and asynchronously returns a `SandboxError` if an error occurred during sandbox execution, or `undefined` otherwise. This is crucial for handling and reporting errors.

```typescript
function extractError(res: Response): Promise<undefined | SandboxError>
```

--------------------------------

### Enumerations

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.1/filesystem/page.mdx

Definitions for filesystem object types.

```APIDOC
## Enumerations

### FileType

Sandbox filesystem object type.

#### Enumeration Members

*   **DIR**: `"dir"` - Filesystem object is a directory.
*   **FILE**: `"file"` - Filesystem object is a file.

```

--------------------------------

### Create Execution Error Object in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.5.0/messaging/page.mdx

Initializes an `ExecutionError` object to capture details about an error that occurred during cell execution. It stores the error's name, value, and a detailed traceback, crucial for debugging.

```typescript
new ExecutionError(
   name: string, 
   value: string, 
   traceback: string): ExecutionError
```

--------------------------------

### Define AuthenticationError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/errors/page.mdx

Thrown when authentication fails. This error class is part of the E2B SDK's custom error hierarchy for handling specific failure scenarios. It accepts a message parameter for detailed error information.

```typescript
class AuthenticationError extends Error {
  constructor(message: any) {
    super(message);
    this.name = "AuthenticationError";
  }
}
```

--------------------------------

### File System Operations

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.2.1/sandbox_async/page.mdx

API endpoints for common file system operations like checking existence, removing, renaming, and creating directories.

```APIDOC
## GET /fs/exists

### Description
Check if a file or a directory exists at the specified path.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/fs/exists

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - Path to a file or a directory.
- **user** (string) - Optional - Run the operation as this user (default: "user").
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **exists** (boolean) - True if the file or directory exists, False otherwise.

### Response Example
```json
{
  "exists": true
}
```

## DELETE /fs/remove

### Description
Remove a file or a directory at the specified path.

### Method
DELETE

### Endpoint
/fs/remove

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - Path to a file or a directory to remove.
- **user** (string) - Optional - Run the operation as this user (default: "user").
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **message** (string) - Confirmation message indicating the removal was successful.

### Response Example
```json
{
  "message": "File removed successfully."
}
```

## PUT /fs/rename

### Description
Rename a file or directory from an old path to a new path.

### Method
PUT

### Endpoint
/fs/rename

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **old_path** (string) - Required - Path to the file or directory to rename.
- **new_path** (string) - Required - New path to the file or directory.
- **user** (string) - Optional - Run the operation as this user (default: "user").
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **entry_info** (object) - Information about the renamed file or directory.
  - **path** (string) - The new path of the entry.
  - **is_dir** (boolean) - True if the entry is a directory, False otherwise.
  - **size** (integer) - The size of the entry in bytes.
  - **mtime** (string) - The last modification time of the entry (ISO 8601 format).

### Response Example
```json
{
  "entry_info": {
    "path": "/new/directory/name",
    "is_dir": true,
    "size": 0,
    "mtime": "2023-10-27T10:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

## POST /fs/make_dir

### Description
Create a new directory and all parent directories along the way if they do not exist.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/fs/make_dir

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **path** (string) - Required - Path to the new directory. For example, '/dirA/dirB' when creating 'dirB'.
- **user** (string) - Optional - Run the operation as this user (default: "user").
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **created** (boolean) - True if the directory was created, False if the directory already exists.

### Response Example
```json
{
  "created": true
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Define SandboxError in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v2.1.4/errors/page.mdx

SandboxError is the base class for all sandbox-related errors within the E2B SDK. It can be instantiated with an optional message and stack trace. This class serves as a common ancestor for more specific sandbox errors, facilitating centralized error handling.

```typescript
class SandboxError {
  constructor(message?: string, stackTrace?: string)
}
```
